summary: Research within this cluster focused on the symbiotic relationship between the dominant North Atlantic brown alga *Ascophyllum nodosum* and the ascomycete fungus *Mycophycias ascophylli*. Microscopic characterization of this symbiotum revealed novel, extensive hyphal networks and multicellular rings that form a continuous radial structure throughout the algal thallus, encircling host cells. These morphological features were identified as the defining basis for a mutualistic association, which was further supported by experimental evidence. The findings underscored the critical role of this symbiosis in the ecological success of *Ascophyllum nodosum* in its North Atlantic habitat.
